what_it_does: Acceptance test script that directly exercises runtime.implement.build_instruction with rich, bare, empty, and malformed payloads to verify it conditionally composes address/symbol/context sections, preserves existing prompt slots, remains byte-for-byte backward-compatible, and never crashes on malformed context.
observations: Tests build_instruction as a pure decision-in/string-out formatter with no suite, embed, subprocess, or network call.
Defines a RICH payload carrying address ui://chat/input, symbols code://App and code://ChatInput, and a bounded context bundle of annotation/chat items.
Asserts the composed prompt contains labelled sections: INDICATED ELEMENT, RELATED CODE, and CONTEXT AT THIS LOCUS.
Asserts backward-compatible bare payload equals an exact expected string that also includes an X15 constitution-hop line.
Includes FAIL-LOUD malformed-context cases: bare string item, dict missing text, integer item, and a non-list context value.
Expects malformed contexts to render valid siblings plus a visible note, without raising any exception.
Uses a module-level PASS counter and a custom check(label, cond) helper instead of a standard test framework.
Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 and inserts the repo root into sys.path before importing build_instruction and STANDARDS_BLOCK.
standouts: Byte-for-byte backward-compat assertion against a hand-written expected_bare prompt string.
Malformed-context tests deliberately feed non-dict and non-list values and require a visible 'malformed'/'skipped' note.
Explicit boundary assertion that build_instruction must stay pure and must not invoke launch/subprocess/claude -p.
conventions: Follows the script-style acceptance-test pattern with inline sys.path setup and environment flag.
Uses uppercase module constants for fixture data.
Embeds extensive docstring/commentary explaining convergence criteria X1/X2/X3/X4/X15.
Breaks standard test-framework conventions by using a global PASS counter and a custom check helper.
concerns: Hard-coded expected_bare string is brittle to any unrelated formatting change in build_instruction.
Couples the X4 acceptance test to the X15 constitution-hop behavior via the exact expected string.
Broad Exception catch to prove non-crashing may hide unexpected errors.
Run command in the docstring hard-codes /home/tim/company/.venv path.
notes: A convergence-X4 acceptance contract that locks build_instruction's prompt-composition behavior for rich payloads and malformed input.
questions: What is the exact current implementation of build_instruction and STANDARDS_BLOCK?
What do the X1/X2/X3/X15 labels map to outside this file?
Is the expected_bare prompt snapshot still accurate if build_instruction formatting changes?
What visible note text does build_instruction emit for malformed context items?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file tests build_instruction's rich-prompt composition, backward compatibility, and malformed-context handling exactly as its docstring describes.
intention_signals: Ensure build_instruction safely and conditionally incorporates new rich payload fields without altering existing behavior, while remaining a pure formatter that degrades loudly on malformed context.
novelty: It asserts an exact expected prompt snapshot that already accounts for a later feature (X15), and it feeds deliberately invalid context items to verify non-crashing visible-note behavior.
contribution: A runnable acceptance contract that documents the expected prompt shape for rich build intents and guards build_instruction against regressions in backward compatibility, section labelling, and malformed-context handling.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test script that directly invokes build_instruction to verify it conditionally composes address, symbol, and context sections into the prompt while preserving existing slots, maintaining byte-for-byte backward compatibility, and handling malformed context bundles without crashing.
intention_for_embedding: To lock the behavior of build_instruction as a pure string formatter that safely slots new rich payload fields and degrades loudly on malformed input.

symbols.does: manual assertion helper that increments a global pass counter and prints an ok/fail status line.
symbols.description: A small inline test helper used in place of a standard framework. It takes a label and a condition, asserts the condition with a descriptive failure message, increments the module-level PASS counter when the check succeeds, and prints a per-check status line to stdout.
